mission.title: Install and Configure Opentelemetry Operator (Opentelemetry) on Kubernetes
mission.description: The OpenTelemetry Operator is a Kubernetes Operator that manages the OpenTelemetry Collector and facilitates auto-instrumentation of workloads using OpenTelemetry libraries. Installing this operator allows you to easily collect and export telemetry data from your applications.

steps.title: Add the OpenTelemetry Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the OpenTelemetry Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration. This will allow you to install the OpenTelemetry Operator using Helm.
```bash
helm repo add open-telemetry https://open-telemetry.github.io/opentelemetry-helm-charts
helm repo update
```

**Important:** The OpenTelemetry Operator requires cert-manager to be installed in your cluster for webhook certificate management. If you do not have cert-manager, you can either install it first (`kubectl apply -f https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/releases/latest/download/cert-manager.yaml`) or disable the cert-manager dependency by adding the flags `--set admissionWebhooks.certManager.enabled=false --set admissionWebhooks.autoGenerateCert.enabled=true` to the install command in the next step.

steps.title: Install the OpenTelemetry Operator
steps.description:
Now, install the OpenTelemetry Operator using Helm. Make sure to specify the namespace where you want to deploy the operator.
```bash
helm install opentelemetry-operator open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator --namespace observability --create-namespace --version v0.145.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the OpenTelemetry Operator is running successfully by listing the pods in the observability namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n observability
```

steps.title: Deploy an OpenTelemetry Collector instance
steps.description:
Create a simple OpenTelemetry Collector instance by applying the following YAML configuration. This will set up a collector that receives telemetry data and exports it to the console.
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: opentelemetry.io/v1beta1
kind: OpenTelemetryCollector
metadata:
  name: simplest
spec:
  config:
    receivers:
      otlp:
        protocols:
          grpc:
            endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4317
          http:
            endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4318
    processors:
      memory_limiter:
        check_interval: 1s
        limit_percentage: 75
        spike_limit_percentage: 15
    exporters:
      debug: {}
    service:
      pipelines:
        traces:
          receivers: [otlp]
          processors: [memory_limiter]
          exporters: [debug]
EOF
```

steps.title: Verify the OpenTelemetry Collector instance
steps.description:
Ensure that the OpenTelemetry Collector instance is running by checking its pod status.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n observability
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the OpenTelemetry Operator and the OpenTelemetry Collector pods running in the 'observability' namespace. You should see the 'simplest' collector instance ready to receive telemetry data.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add open-telemetry https://open-telemetry.github.io/opentelemetry-helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install opentelemetry-operator open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator --namespace observability --create-namespace --version v0.145.0

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: opentelemetry.io/v1beta1
kind: OpenTelemetryCollector
metadata:
  name: simplest
spec:
  config:
    receivers:
      otlp:
        protocols:
          grpc:
            endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4317
          http:
            endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4318
    processors:
      memory_limiter:
        check_interval: 1s
        limit_percentage: 75
        spike_limit_percentage: 15
    exporters:
      debug: {}
    service:
      pipelines:
        traces:
          receivers: [otlp]
          processors: [memory_limiter]
          exporters: [debug]
EOF

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
To cleanly remove the OpenTelemetry Operator, use the following command to uninstall the Helm release from the specified namespace. This will delete the operator and its associated resources.
```bash
helm uninstall opentelemetry-operator --namespace observability
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
After uninstalling the Helm release, you should clean up any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) that were created. This command will delete the OpenTelemetry CRDs from your cluster.
```bash
kubectl delete crd opentelemetrycollectors.opentelemetry.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
If you no longer need the 'observability' namespace, you can delete it. This will remove all resources within that namespace. Verify that the namespace has been deleted successfully.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace observability
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it's a good practice to back up your current configurations and resources. You can export the current OpenTelemetry Collector configuration as follows:
```bash
kubectl get otelcol simplest -n observability -o yaml > otelcol-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts, then upgrade the OpenTelemetry Operator to the desired version. Replace 'v0.146.0' with the target version you wish to upgrade to.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade opentelemetry-operator open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator --namespace observability --version v0.146.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, check the status of the OpenTelemetry Operator pods to ensure they are running the new version correctly.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n observability
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you notice that your OpenTelemetry Collector pod is in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for more information on the failure.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n observability
``` 
Look for errors in the logs that may indicate configuration issues or resource constraints.

troubleshooting.title: Collector not receiving data
troubleshooting.description:
If the OpenTelemetry Collector is not receiving data, verify that the application is correctly configured to send telemetry data to the collector's endpoints. Check the collector's configuration for any misconfigurations:
```bash
kubectl get otelcol simplest -n observability -o yaml
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version conflict
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter a version conflict during the Helm upgrade, ensure that the version you are trying to upgrade to is compatible with your current Kubernetes version. You can check the available versions with:
```bash
helm search repo open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator --versions
```

troubleshooting.title: Namespace does not exist
troubleshooting.description:
If you receive an error that the 'observability' namespace does not exist, ensure that you are specifying the correct namespace in your commands. List all namespaces to verify:
```bash
kubectl get namespaces
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy the OpenTelemetry Operator. The operator requires cert-manager for webhook certificate management. If cert-manager is not available, use the --set admissionWebhooks.certManager.enabled=false --set admissionWebhooks.autoGenerateCert.enabled=true flags during installation.
